action.reasoning: Observation: iter_25 PCA+Ridge validation 0.273 < baseline 0.285, so linear model is best so far (test mean_r2 0.304). Remaining time ~21 min, 5 iterations. Linear model already captures most linear variance; non-linear MLP may boost. Analysis: A small MLP (256,128) trained for 3-5 epochs on full dataset can capture interactions and potentially improve R². Training 5 epochs takes ~5-7 min, validation ~2-3 min, total <10 min. We can implement early stopping based on val R² on a subsample to quickly decide if it beats linear model. Decision: write mlp_train.py to train a simple ClimateMLP (added to model.py) on full training data with memmap streaming, evaluate safe val R², save best model if > 0.285. Update predict.py to load MLP if checkpoint exists, else fallback to linear. Run training in this iteration, check result, then submit eval if improved.
